C[Nogravity: Good day Ben, ... Your new article on your blog is absolutely amazing. I have found myself as a bad teacher of Go, just like you said, I was trying to explain everything in five minutes, and the rest was about teaching what i learned by two years of my playing. ... But why i am writing this to you. I live in small town and there is noone to play with. So my only solution is to share the game with others. How should i start? Write something on big paper and sit in park and just wait for people to come? ... How you started? ...
Nogravity: Another question is about Undo while teaching ... i am always horrified when i see anyone place a stone and than simply place it on another. Shouldnt you try to erase this at begining ?
C[BenGoZen: In terms of starting, I recommend going to a place where a lot of people come and go: like a coffee shop or something.
BenGoZen: Then go ahead and pull out your board and start reviewing a game on the board.
BenGoZen: Over time people will see you playing, and will probably be curious.
BenGoZen: And then you have one of two choices
BenGoZen: Any time you see someone look over, you can ask them if they'd like to learn. In that case, I would have a 9x9 board ready to do so.
BenGoZen: Or, you can put up a little sign / paper that welcomes people to interrupt you and learn about the game,.
BenGoZen: My experience is primarily at festivals like China or Japan Festivals where people are already interested to learn about the culture, so it's very easy to get people to sit down.
BenGoZen: When I've had more experience doing it in random places though, I'll be sure to let you know.
BenGoZen: To your second question, when it comes to teaching games, don't worry about undo.
BenGoZen: You want to let them undo as much as they want because that means they are learning.
BenGoZen: And because you are not trying to win, it shouldn't matter to you anyways. Haha.
BenGoZen: Only when they start wanting to play serious games and get stronger will undo matter.
BenGoZen: But otherwise, if it's for fun, let them undo as much as they want. =D

fredrikkoehler: Would love to try Svijany, not avaiable in Sweden though. Alcohol is actually monopolized here in Sweden by a company run by the state, ""systembolaget"".
